responsibilities: Develop and run high-fidelity CFD simulations to predict fluid flow, pressure drop, and thermal performance of components
Generate high-quality computational meshes using structured and unstructured techniques to balance accuracy and solve time
Apply turbulence and heat transfer models to simulate real-world operating conditions across diverse engineering systems
Analyze simulation results and translate findings into actionable design recommendations for mechanical engineering teams
Validate CFD models against experimental test data to ensure simulation accuracy and build confidence in predictions
Automate simulation workflows using scripting languages to accelerate design of experiments and parametric studies
Collaborate with thermal and mechanical design engineers to optimize cooling systems, ducting, and airflow pathways
Present simulation findings and technical recommendations to cross-functional stakeholders and leadership
Run simulations on high-performance computing clusters to manage large-scale, computationally intensive models
